title: raxis | 95 Dataplane bottlenecks (p50/p95/p99 across iter61 wire-sites)

panels.title: iter61 dataplane bottleneck wire-sites — p50 / p95 / p99 per pivot
Audit chain stage duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by stage
panels.description: Per-stage walk-cost in the on-disk audit chain. Source: `AuditChainStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-02`). Pivots on `stage` ∈ {`load_segment`, `verify_segment`, `walk_chain`}. A regression in `verify_segment` = signature batching slowed; in `walk_chain` = the chain grew faster than the bounded ring expected.

panels.title: Store SQL query duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by query_class (top-N)
panels.description: Per-query-class SQLite read latency on the dashboard read path. Source: `StoreQueryDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-01` + `-07`). The `query_class` lexicon is closed (`crates/store/src/observability.rs::QUERY_CLASSES`) so a sustained spike on one class localises a slow dashboard refresh to the underlying SQL — e.g. `audit_chain_walk` p99 climbing means the chain grew, `policy_snapshot` p99 climbing means the bundle projection grew with operator count.

panels.title: FSM event→commit duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by fsm_kind
panels.description: Wall-clock from FSM event-emit to audit-sink commit, pivoted on `fsm_kind` ∈ {`session`, `initiative`, `task`}. Source: `FsmTransitionDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-03`), wired in `kernel/src/notifications/sink.rs::bridge_audit_to_metric`. A regression localises a slow lifecycle to the FSM that owns the transition rather than the generic audit-append path. `from_state`/`to_state` labels are also present on the underlying samples — pivot on them in ad-hoc queries when one fsm_kind shows a tail spike.

panels.title: Git worktree stage duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by stage
panels.description: Host-side git provisioning sub-stage timings. Source: `GitWorktreeStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-04`), wired in `crates/worktree-provision/src/lib.rs`. Pivots on `stage` ∈ {`clone`, `fetch`, `checkout`, `verify`}. Both success AND error paths emit (the bottleneck signal is the error-path histogram, not just the happy path) — pivot on `outcome` in ad-hoc queries to separate slow-clones from fail-fast clones.

panels.title: Gateway stage duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by stage
panels.description: Per-stage gateway-internal timing. Source: `GatewayStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-06`). This iter61 commit wires `tproxy_admit` (transparent-proxy admission verdict in `crates/egress-admission/src/lib.rs`); the spec has slots for `dns`, `tls`, and `first_byte` which are deferred to a follow-up because `reqwest`'s public surface does not currently expose hooks at those sub-stages. Pivot on `outcome` in ad-hoc queries to split `ok` vs `denied` (the `denied` series is the rate-limit / policy-deny signal).

panels.title: IPC frame stage duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by stage / channel / direction
panels.description: Per-stage bincode-frame timing across the kernel ↔ substrate vsock chokepoint. Source: `IpcFrameStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-05`), wired in `crates/ipc/src/frame.rs`. Pivots on `stage` ∈ {`encode`, `write`, `read`, `decode`}. A regression in `encode`/`decode` = bincode work grew (e.g. larger payload variants); in `write`/`read` = vsock back-pressure or the peer's drain stalled. Pivot on `direction` (`tx`/`rx`) and `channel` (`intent`/`ksb`/`audit`/`notify`) in ad-hoc queries when one stage shows a spike.
